page: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and compression tasks. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which drive top-tier results in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and stress-ng multi-core benchmarks also rank in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is moderate, falling into the average tier.

The server demonstrates strong capabilities in large-scale data processing and database operations, as evidenced by top-tier Redis SET operations and Passmark database benchmarks. For artificial intelligence workloads, the platform achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds for large 70B models, though text generation speeds for smaller models are relatively weak. The low memory-to-core ratio makes this instance highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and parallelized CPU-bound computations.
description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 192 dedicated vCPUs and 96 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 384.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The server lacks local storage but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks highlight top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and strong database operations, though single-core performance remains average. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, making it highly efficient for parallelized, compute-heavy tasks. This instance is best suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and compute-intensive batch processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for database operations and parallel processing.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density compute platform with 192 dedicated cores designed for parallel processing, multi-threaded workloads, and high-throughput database operations.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors
384.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Hardware acceleration for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions
Optimized for parallel batch processing, web serving, and databases
